note: Publicly marketed powered-land listings and market signals compiled from broker pages, press releases, and news coverage. Coordinates are approximate (city/county-level) unless precision says 'exact'. Prices shown only where publicly stated.

sites.name: Childress Powered Land (Hwy 287)
sites.power_notes: Multiple 345kV and 138kV lines traverse the area; grid power via the Tesla substation. Flat site, fully outside floodplain.
sites.broker: CBRE (Brant Bernet, Chris Herrmann, Phoebe Bernet)

sites.name: South Dallas DC Development (Waxahachie)
sites.power_notes: 138kV and 345kV infrastructure upgrades approved by ERCOT (Oncor); pathway to hundreds of MW. Water agreement + TPDES sewer permit in place.
sites.broker: CBRE (Brant Bernet, Chris Herrmann, Phoebe Bernet)

sites.name: West Texas Panhandle 5,000-Acre AI Campus
sites.power_notes: Three power lanes: 250MW behind-the-meter ramp from mid-2026, 250MW wind offtake from Jul 2026, high-pressure gas line supporting 500MW on-site generation in 12 months (ramp to 1GW). Own 345kV frontage; can loop into SPP.
sites.fiber_notes: Zayo and AT&T fiber backbone; municipal water access

sites.name: North Abilene Tract A (1,450± ac)
sites.power_notes: Between two major data centers under construction near Abilene (Stargate corridor).

sites.name: North Abilene Tract B (1,630± ac, on-site substation)
sites.power_notes: Existing electric substation and transmission lines in NW portion of property; between two data centers under construction.

sites.name: Pineywoods (855 ac, East Texas)
sites.power_notes: ~640 buildable acres served by three HV transmission lines (345kV, 138kV, 69kV) and five major interstate natural gas pipelines; supports front-of-meter and behind-the-meter.

sites.name: DFW 1GW Ground-Lease (4×250MW blocks)
sites.power_notes: 1GW ground-lease opportunity subdivided into four 250MW blocks scheduled to energize 2028.

sites.name: DFW Strategic 1,500-Acre Hyperscale Parcel
sites.power_notes: Positioned for hyperscale campus development; 345kV transmission frontage tracts in broker portfolio.

sites.name: 88-Acre Substation-Adjacent Site (Waco–Austin corridor)
sites.power_notes: Substation adjacency; outside city jurisdiction (ETJ advantages). Marketed for crypto/AI training workloads.

sites.name: West Texas 82-Acre Edge/Modular AI Site
sites.power_notes: Marketed for edge or modular AI deployment.

sites.name: Aquila Site (Hill County, 800+ ac)
sites.power_notes: Four contiguous parcels under contract at ~$100k/acre (~14× appraisal) for proposed data center development.

sites.name: Amazon Data Services — Bastrop County (1,300+ ac acquired)
sites.power_notes: One of the largest Central Texas land assemblies tied to hyperscale development (May 2026).

sites.name: Tract — Caldwell County Technology Park (1,515 ac, multi-GW)
sites.power_notes: Master-planned multi-gigawatt data center technology park between Austin and San Antonio.

sites.name: CloudBurst — San Marcos (706 ac, 1.2GW approved)
sites.power_notes: 1.2GW campus approvals; ~$14.5B projected investment.

sites.name: APR Energy — Pampa (800 ac contracted + 500 ac ROFR)
sites.power_notes: On-site gas turbines planned; phase 1 targeted early 2026.

sites.name: Advanced Energy & Intelligence Campus — Amarillo (aka Project Matador/HyperGrid, ~7,570 ac)
sites.power_notes: Fermi America / Texas Tech University System campus on a 99-year ground lease; ~6GW of natural gas generation already TCEQ-permitted (Nov 2025) en route to a targeted 17GW total (11GW natural gas, 4.4GW nuclear, plus solar/battery); 18M sq ft of data centers planned.
sites.status: In development (TCEQ preliminary approval for first 6GW clean natural gas facility, Nov 2025)

sites.name: Black Mountain Power — Bowie County (600 ac acquired)
sites.power_notes: Fort Worth company purchased ~600 ac (Mar 2026) for data center development.

sites.name: Stargate (Crusoe/OpenAI) — Abilene anchor campus
sites.power_notes: Anchor hyperscale AI campus driving the Abilene powered-land corridor.

sites.name: Galaxy Helios — West Texas (1.6GW+, 1,500+ ac)
sites.power_notes: Former bitcoin mine converted to AI data center campus; grown to 1.6GW+ in 2026 — the template for West Texas powered-land conversions.

sites.name: Stream Data Centers — Graham Project (ex-Plug Power, 164MW interconnect)
sites.power_notes: Plug Power sold its Graham, TX land and associated 164MW of grid interconnection assets to Stream Data Centers for up to $76.5M ($50M at closing, up to $26.5M tied to confirmed load capacity in the final Texas utility interconnection agreement).
sites.status: Sale agreement signed; closing expected on/about Jul 31, 2026

sites.name: Big Digital Energy / 10NetZero JV — Hood County Site
sites.power_notes: Powered industrial site with 17MW operational power today, expandable to 111MW grid power pending ERCOT validation. On-site natural gas pipelines (two 12-inch, one 20-inch) allow behind-the-meter generation for a buildout to up to 311MW total. 30,000+ sq ft of existing structures to be repurposed for AI data center use.
sites.status: 50/50 JV + signed letter of intent to acquire (Jul 6, 2026)

sites.name: MARA — Matagorda County Site (ex-HIF USA, up to 2GW)
sites.power_notes: Definitive agreement with HIF USA for a large-scale powered land site ~90 miles SW of Houston; access to up to 1GW of grid capacity by Oct 2027, up to 2GW by Apr 2028. To be developed with Starwood Digital Ventures for HPC/flexible compute including Bitcoin mining.

sites.name: AmpZ Champion / EPG Champion — Angelina County (former Southland Paper Mill)
sites.power_notes: Memorandum of option for ~1,041 acres across multiple abstracts/surveys at the former Southland Paper Mill site; nearby land could expand the project to ~4,000 acres. Part of the site to be used as a sound/wetland buffer. Estimated $1.05B private capital investment if finalized.
sites.status: Option agreement / under development and community negotiation (Jul 2026)

sites.name: Tract — Medina Technology Park (265 ac, 500MW)
sites.power_notes: Shovel-ready data center campus off CR 4516/US-90W, served by Medina Electric Cooperative and East Medina County SUD; site energization targeted mid-2028; adjacent to an existing Microsoft campus in Castroville. One of three San Antonio-area Tract sites (with Caldwell County and a third location) totaling ~3,500 ac / 5,220MW across the region.

sites.name: New Era Energy & Digital — TCDC Campus (Ector County, 438+54 ac, 1GW+)
sites.power_notes: Flagship hyperscale AI/HPC campus; 438 acres owned plus a 54-acre corridor pending; on-site natural gas-fired power generation with carbon capture (up to 250,000 metric tons CO2/yr); phased buildout toward 1+GW. Non-binding JV letter of intent with Stream Data Centers for development/financing announced Apr 2026.
sites.status: In development (JV LOI with Stream Data Centers, Apr 1 2026; underwriters' option funding secured Apr 14 2026)

sites.name: Infrakey — Lacy Lakeview Data District (520 ac, 925MW)
sites.power_notes: Public-private data center district formed with the City of Lacy Lakeview on farmland west of Elm Mott; includes a planned ~1.2GW gas-fired power plant; ~$10B projected investment. Facing active community opposition and an annexation/jurisdiction dispute with the City of Waco.
sites.status: Announced / in development; community and jurisdictional disputes ongoing as of Jun 2026

sites.name: Helena Harbor Industrial Park
sites.power_notes: 10-inch, 800 PSI steel natural gas line installed on-site (service established Jan 2015). Publicly owned, flood-protected industrial park behind the main Mississippi River levee at river mile 652 AHP, ~65 mi south of Memphis; direct barge docking on a 2.25-mile, 9-ft-deep slackwater harbor/turning basin; 60-ton crane on site.
sites.status: Available; container yard construction approved Dec 2024, additional rail/water supply improvements expected 2027

sites.name: Belwood Industrial Site
sites.power_notes: Entergy substation directly adjacent across River Terminal Road. Rail via Natchez Railway (connects to CN spur in Brookhaven). Adjacent to Natchez-Adams County Port (rail, liquid loading dock, 250-ton pedestal crane). ~1,500 ft Mississippi River frontage; protected by a Delta Regional Authority-funded 8,000-ft ring levee (~$1.27M). One source cites 214 acres for a related parcel; unresolved discrepancy with the 110-acre figure.
sites.status: County-owned; Adams County has granted a purchase option on part of the property to a prospective client (as of mid-2020s reporting) — reconfirm current availability

sites.name: Madison Parish Port — adjacent 500-acre tract
sites.power_notes: Electricity from Entergy Louisiana, natural gas from CenterPoint Energy (no specific substation voltage or pipeline tap size confirmed). Core 266-acre port site has a 400-ft concrete T-dock for barge loading/unloading and certified truck scales; a separate 35-acre fenced sub-parcel has a 10,000 sq ft warehouse plus 5 smaller buildings.

sites.name: West Memphis Rail Port Logistics Park + Port Terminal
sites.power_notes: "Multi-megawatt power supply" stated for the Rail Port Logistics Park (no specific MW figure); 2.7 MGD excess water / 1 MGD excess wastewater capacity. Adjacent Watco-operated port terminal (1,400 ac build-to-suit + 10 ac general purpose) has barge dock, 100-ton boom crane, heavy-haul road access, and a new ~13,500-ft heavy-haul rail lead to the St. Francis levee base; interchange with Union Pacific, BNSF, CSX, Canadian National, Norfolk Southern. No specific substation or gas-pipeline tap named in sources.
sites.status: Available; part of a 2025 West Memphis industrial-growth push
sites.broker: Watco (port terminal, sales@watco.com); City of West Memphis Economic Development (logistics park)

sites.name: Rosedale-Bolivar County Port
sites.power_notes: Port property totals 1,200 acres with 1,123 acres stated available (largest contiguous tract up to 500 ac); separately the adjoining 270-acre Rosedale Industrial Park (landside of the levee) has 200+ acres available. No substation or gas-pipeline detail found in sources — needs follow-up.
sites.status: Available; actively marketed by the port commission

sites.name: Ingevity Crossett (former GP Crossett pine-chemicals plant)
sites.power_notes: Oleochemical/tall-oil-rosin plant using natural-gas-fired hot oil heaters for product separation (explicit gas dependency; tap size/operator not stated in sources). Co-located within the large Georgia-Pacific Crossett industrial/mill complex, implying shared rail and water infrastructure — inference, not independently sourced.
sites.status: Production ceased August 2024 (~70 employees affected, operations relocated to North Charleston, SC); no sale/redevelopment news found yet

sites.name: Beale Infrastructure "Project Blue" Data Center (TEP-anchored)
sites.city: Tucson (unincorporated, SE Employment and Logistics Center)
sites.power_notes: 10-year Energy Supply Agreement with Tucson Electric Power (TEP) approved by the Arizona Corporation Commission 4-1 on Dec 3, 2025; TEP to provide 138kV service; customer pays full large-user rate, no discounts; developer responsible for dedicated infrastructure; load ramps over 18 months to full 286MW draw by 2028. Full build-out (~10 buildings, 2M sq ft) could reach up to 600MW.
sites.status: ESA approved by ACC (Dec 2025); Pima County land-use deal approved Dec 17, 2025; construction phase 1 targeted 2026-2029, online target May 2027

sites.name: Project Midway Data Center
sites.power_notes: Served by Electrical District No. 3 (ED3), not APS/SRP — the ~225MW need is described as nearly all of ED3's current capacity; on-site substation planned (sources disagree on substation footprint, 10 ac vs 50 ac); backup generators beside each of 5 planned data-center buildings; ~100MW proposed to come from the adjacent "Project Bella" 480MW-gas/440MW-battery facility sited directly south.
sites.status: Rezoning (GR to I-3 + PAD) approved by Pinal County Board of Supervisors 5-0, Nov 19, 2025

sites.name: Energy Generation & Technology Campus + Griffin Energy Project
sites.city: South of Maricopa (data-center campus); SE of Stanfield (power site)
sites.power_notes: Dedicated 2,685-acre power campus near Stanfield ("Griffin Energy Project," developer Copia Power) to feed the 495-acre data-center/tech campus south of Maricopa: up to 550MW solar, up to 550MW battery storage, and up to 500MW gas-fired generation. Combined project investment ~$9.5-10B.
sites.status: Comprehensive plan/rezoning approved by Pinal County Board of Supervisors, Nov 19, 2025

sites.name: Sandow Lakes / Xebec Megasite (former Alcoa aluminum plant)
sites.power_notes: ~1.5GW of existing ERCOT-connected capacity today, anchored by the legacy Alcoa electrical backbone that powered the former aluminum smelter; an additional 1.2GW Oncor transmission substation was expected to deliver capacity in Q3 2025, with further substations planned to reach ~3.9GW by 2028.
sites.status: Acquired by Xebec Holdings (SLR Property I LP) from Alcoa in Nov 2021 for $240M; Phase 1 (Advanced Manufacturing Logistix Campus, up to 35M sq ft) under construction, targeted ready-for-occupancy Q3 2026 — bulk of the 33,000-acre site remains available for additional build-to-suit development

sites.name: Madison County Mega Site (Entergy Qualified Site Program)
sites.power_notes: Mississippi PSC-approved $57M Entergy substation (in service ~Dec 2021) initially serves up to 80MW with capacity to provide up to 300MW in the future via three independent transmission sources; part of Entergy Mississippi's Qualified Site Program.
sites.status: Certified/qualified site; 765+ acres qualified (Madison County Economic Development Authority owns 325 ac, ~875 ac under option); near the Nissan Canton plant off I-55
sites.broker: Madison County Economic Development Authority / Entergy Mississippi

sites.name: Beauregard Airport Industrial Site
sites.power_notes: A natural gas pipeline runs across the site; dual-feed redundant electric service from Cleco and Beauregard Electric (Cleco's Western Louisiana region).
sites.status: LED-certified, shovel-ready; potable water on-site, city sewer nearby; Timber Rock rail spur connects to the Kansas City Southern main line through DeRidder; frontage on US Hwy 190
sites.broker: Louisiana Economic Development / Beauregard Economic Development

sites.name: International Paper Red River Containerboard Mill
sites.power_notes: Former containerboard mill (~800,000 tons/yr annualized capacity); adjoins the Natchitoches Parish Port, a multimodal industrial center on the Red River Waterway with barge, rail (4,000+ ft of Kansas City Southern Railroad frontage), and US Hwy 71 highway access. No specific gas-tap or substation figures published yet — flagging for follow-up.
sites.status: Closure announced Feb 13, 2025; operations ceased end of April 2025 (~674 jobs); Louisiana Economic Development stated it is working to locate a buyer to bring the site back into production

sites.name: Pima County Aerospace Research Campus (TEP transmission-served)
sites.power_notes: Tucson Electric Power is building a new 3-mile, 138kV transmission line linking its existing system to a proposed Franco Wash Switchyard to serve the campus; Arizona Corporation Commission approved TEP's Certificate of Environmental Compatibility in Jan 2024, construction targeted complete 2026.
sites.status: Marketed by Sun Corridor Inc. as an available business campus for aerospace/advanced-manufacturing tenants; TEP transmission project underway to serve it
